course_title: Environmental Health Seminar: Wearables in Public Hlth.Rsch.
subject: EH
course_description: Various topics by GDEH faculty. Check OPUS/Atlas for current topics and descriptions.
course_notes: Topic: Wearables in Public Health Research. Wearable devices are useful tools that hold great promise for advancing the biomedical and public health sciences. To effectively adapt and operate these tools for experimental or observational research, researchers must first understand important principles in the collection, processing, and analysis of wearables data collection. The main goal of this course is to equip students with core competencies in the current ecosystem of wearables research, including a hands-on component to practice real-world data collection and analysis. Content experts will introduce and engage students in a variety of cutting-edge applications and considerations for wearable devices. Upon completion of this course, students will be equipped with several R-based statistical tools for analyzing and visualizing wearables data and be able to read and interpret scientific literature utilizing these approaches. Students should have completed prior coursework in biostatistics prior to enrolling in this course or be able to demonstrate some level of proficiency with R programming. Students with no R programming background must first complete a self-guided online (free) course by Applied Epi, here: https://courses.appliedepi.org/intror/course/intro_en.html. While not required, additional prior coursework in biology, physiology, and/or epidemiology are highly encouraged."
